daily_brief: The World Health Organization declared the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo a public health emergency of international concern on Sunday, with approximately 300 suspected cases and 88 deaths from a rare Bundibugyo strain for which no approved vaccine exists [BBC], [Globe and Mail]. The virus has already spread to Uganda, raising fears of regional transmission. Meanwhile, G7 finance ministers convene in Paris today amid sharp disagreements over trade policy, China relations, and the economic fallout from the ongoing US-Israel war with Iran [RFI], [Le Monde]. That conflict showed no signs of resolution as President Trump warned Iran the 'clock is ticking' after Tehran rejected US demands as excessive [BBC], [France 24]. A drone strike on a nuclear power plant in Abu Dhabi underscored the fragility of the ceasefire, with no group claiming responsibility [RNZ], [Globe and Mail]. In a significant development for US-China relations, Beijing agreed to purchase $17 billion annually in US agricultural products following the Trump-Xi summit, offering relief to American farmers caught in the trade war [The Hindu]. Japanese government bond yields hit 29-year highs at 2.8% as fiscal concerns mount amid the Middle East crisis [Nikkei Asia].

top_stories.headline: WHO Declares Ebola Outbreak in DR Congo a Global Health Emergency
bullets: The WHO declared the Ebola outbreak in DR Congo's Ituri province a public health emergency of international concern, citing approximately 300 suspected cases and 88 deaths
The outbreak involves the rare Bundibugyo strain of Ebola, for which no approved vaccines or treatments currently exist
The virus has spread beyond Congo's borders, with two confirmed cases in Uganda including one fatality in Kampala
Health officials warn the outbreak may be 'potentially much larger' than detected, having circulated undetected for at least four weeks in a region marked by mining activity and armed conflict

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasized the technical aspects of the WHO declaration and provided detailed epidemiological context

sources.name: The Guardian
sources.framing_note: Earlier reporting focused on initial case counts before WHO declaration

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Highlighted the lack of approved treatments and the speed of WHO's response without usual procedures

top_stories.headline: G7 Finance Ministers Meet in Paris Amid Deep Divisions on Trade and Iran War Fallout
bullets: Finance ministers and central bankers from G7 nations began two-day talks in Paris on Monday to address divergent positions on trade, China policy, and economic disruptions from the Iran conflict
France, holding the G7 presidency, acknowledged significant disagreements with the US over new 15 percent tariff surcharges imposed earlier this year
Discussions will address America's budget deficit, Europe's innovation challenges, Chinese industrial overcapacity, and critical mineral supply chains
French Finance Minister Roland Lescure stated 'the way the global economy has been developing for more than a decade is clearly unsustainable'

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Detailed coverage of French hosting role and specific policy disagreements

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Article title available but full content not accessible

top_stories.headline: Trump Warns Iran as Ceasefire Talks Stall; Drone Strikes Hit Gulf States
bullets: President Trump warned Iran the 'clock is ticking' after Tehran rejected US ceasefire demands as excessive, with talks continuing through Pakistani mediation
Iran announced formation of a new body to manage the Strait of Hormuz, which Tehran has effectively closed and seeks to charge ships to traverse
Drone strikes hit targets in Saudi Arabia and the UAE on Sunday, including a fire at the Barakah Nuclear Power Plant in Abu Dhabi; no casualties or radiation release reported
Iranian media reported negotiations at an 'impasse' while Trump said Iran was 'dying to sign' a deal but failing to honor terms

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Balanced presentation of both US and Iranian positions on negotiations

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Emphasized ongoing diplomatic exchanges and Iranian response to US proposals

sources.name: RNZ
sources.framing_note: Focused on the nuclear plant attack and UAE security response

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Connected attack to broader ceasefire fragility and regional tensions

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Detailed Iranian announcement of Strait of Hormuz management body

top_stories.headline: China Agrees to $17 Billion Annual US Agricultural Purchases Following Trump-Xi Summit
bullets: China committed to purchasing US agricultural products at an annualized rate of $17 billion per year for 2026-2028, the White House announced Sunday
The deal includes restored market access for US beef and resumed poultry imports from bird-flu-free states
The agreement follows President Trump's return from a summit in Beijing aimed at easing trade war impacts on American farmers
American farmers face additional pressure from the Iran war, which has restricted global fertilizer supplies through the Strait of Hormuz and driven up prices

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Provided detailed terms of the agreement and noted Beijing had not immediately confirmed the specifics

top_stories.headline: Japan Bond Yields Hit 29-Year High as Fiscal and Inflation Concerns Mount
bullets: Ten-year Japanese government bond yields climbed to 2.8% on Monday, the highest level since October 1996
Investors are selling sovereign debt amid inflation pressures and fiscal concerns exacerbated by the Middle East conflict
The Japanese government has begun deliberating whether to compile a supplementary budget to address rising fuel costs
The yen dropped to its lowest level since the last currency intervention

sources.name: Nikkei Asia
sources.framing_note: Connected bond market moves to broader Middle East conflict disruptions and domestic policy considerations

Mali's military junta launches airstrikes against rebel alliance as Russian mercenaries support counteroffensive
